contract.name: v2/awards/funding.md
contract.description: Lists federal account funding records for a given award including obligated and outlay amounts by reporting period.

award_id.description: Identifier of the award to inspect (generated award id preferred, surrogate integer id also accepted).
award_id.constraints: Required. Accepts generated award identifier strings or surrogate integer ids. Arrays, nulls, and decimal numbers return 422 validation errors. Unknown ids respond 200 with empty results.

limit.description: Maximum number of records to return on a page.
limit.constraints: Optional. Defaults to 10. Accepts integers from 1-100 inclusive; numeric strings (including leading zeros or surrounding whitespace) are coerced. Values below 1 or above 100 return 422. Null, decimal strings, or strings containing non-digits return 400.

page.description: 1-based page index to fetch when results exceed the limit.
page.constraints: Optional. Defaults to 1. Must resolve to an integer greater than or equal to 1. Numeric strings (including leading zeros or whitespace) are coerced. Values below 1 return 422. Decimal or non-numeric strings and null return 400. Very large page numbers return 200 with empty results.

sort.description: Field used to order results (must match a response field name).
sort.constraints: Optional. Enumerated values: account_title, awarding_agency_name, disaster_emergency_fund_code, federal_account, funding_agency_name, gross_outlay_amount, object_class, program_activity, reporting_fiscal_date, transaction_obligated_amount. When omitted the API sorts by account_title descending and breaks ties by reporting_fiscal_date ascending, contrary to the documentation. Invalid or null values return 400.

order.constraints: Optional. Valid values: asc, desc (case sensitive). Defaults to desc even when sort is omitted; supplying order without sort reverses the implicit account_title ordering. Null, numeric, or other strings return 400.

results.description: Array of funding records for the requested award. Each record provides federal_account and account_title strings; funding and awarding agency ids, names, toptier ids, and slugs; object_class and object_class_name strings; program_activity_code and program_activity_name strings which may be null; disaster_emergency_fund_code string values such as 'Q' or 'N' or null; transaction_obligated_amount and gross_outlay_amount numbers that may be null or negative; reporting_fiscal_year/quarter/month numbers that may be null; and is_quarterly_submission boolean.

page_metadata.description: Pagination metadata containing integer page, optional next and previous integers (null when unavailable), and booleans hasNext and hasPrevious.

results.account_title: Salaries and Expenses, Social Security Administration
Salaries and Expenses, Social Security Administration
Office of the Inspector General, Social Security Administration
Office of the Inspector General, Social Security Administration

body.detail: Invalid value in 'limit'. '5.5' is not a valid type (integer)

quirks: Omitting sort causes the service to order results by account_title descending and break ties by reporting_fiscal_date ascending, not by reporting_fiscal_date desc as documented.
Supplying order without sort flips the implicit account_title sort direction while keeping the same field.
Null monetary fields (transaction_obligated_amount or gross_outlay_amount) are common and sort ahead of numeric values when ordering desc.
Requests for nonexistent award ids return 200 with empty results and page_metadata.hasNext=false instead of an error.
Integer-like strings for limit/page (including leading zeros or whitespace) are coerced, while decimal strings or non-digits trigger 400 errors.
risks: Clients must handle null and negative monetary values when aggregating funding data.
Empty 200 responses for unknown award ids may mask lookup errors unless callers check for results length.
Sorting with many duplicate keys may yield non-deterministic secondary ordering and repeats across pages.
gaps: The API does not expose total record counts, so the precise number of pages must be discovered iteratively.
Meaning of disaster_emergency_fund_code letter values (e.g., 'Q', 'N') is not described by the API responses or documentation.

probes.notes: Default pagination returned 10 rows with a next page pointer.
Limit reduced the page size to three records.
Pagination advanced to page 2 with previous pointer.
Ascending sort placed the most negative obligations first.
Invalid order string rejected with a 400 error.
Omitting award_id triggers a 422 validation error.
Unknown award id returns an empty result set with 200 OK.
Limit values below 1 are rejected with 422.
Numeric strings with leading zeros are coerced to integer limits.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"account_title":"Office of the Inspector General, Social Security Administration","reporting_fiscal_year":2017},...],"page_metadata":{"page":1,"hasNext":false}}

probes.notes: Supplying order without sort reverses the implicit account_title ordering while keeping the same field.

mismatches: Documentation states disaster_emergency_fund_code is boolean, but live responses return letter codes such as 'Q' or 'N' or null.
Documentation marks program_activity_code/program_activity_name as required, yet live data includes null values for both fields.
Documentation lists reporting_fiscal_date descending as the default sort, but the API defaults to account_title descending with older fiscal periods first.
gaps: The API does not expose total record counts, so the precise number of pages must be discovered iteratively.
Meaning of disaster_emergency_fund_code letter values (e.g., 'Q', 'N') is not described by the API responses or documentation.
risks: Clients must handle null and negative monetary values when aggregating funding data.
Empty 200 responses for unknown award ids may mask lookup errors unless callers check for results length.
Sorting with many duplicate keys may yield non-deterministic secondary ordering and repeats across pages.
